description: Short Bio of Solly Adam (Born in Gujarat, in 1945): Solly (Suleman) Adam played League Cricket in Yorkshire for nearly four decades, mentoring and nurturing hundreds of young talents. These included over 400 cricketers, primarily from South Asia (India and Pakistan) but also some local Yorkshiremen, several of whom went on to become international cricket stars and legends. Solly played a key role in securing Sachin Tendulkar's signing with Yorkshire Cricket in 1992, a historic move that transformed Yorkshire Cricket by promoting greater unity and inclusivity. The role and significance of Solly for cricket in England has been recognised widely during his active career and was lauded by the media. The contribution of Solly to cricket has also been recognised by the office of Her Majesty Queen Elizabeth, who bestowed him with the rarest opportunity to be a part of the Garden Tea Party at Buckingham Palace in 2015. The story of his life and mission would not only be an inspiration for today’s generation but would also instil universal brotherhood for the peace of the world at large. In this era of globalisation and cosmopolitanism, the spirit of inclusive humanity as an ideal that Solly Adam emulated and cherished for his life is more relevant and significant today, than ever.
